results.possibleIdentity: A. R. Gruss was an optical engineer associated with the Perkin Elmer Corporation during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Based on professional records and historical documentation from the organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), Gruss was a technical collaborator who offered expertise in the field of optics. During this period, Gruss was based in New Rochelle, New York, while employed by Perkin-Elmer, a leading manufacturer of analytical instruments and optical systems (known for its headquarters in nearby Norwalk, Connecticut). As a participant in the E.A.T. network, Gruss was part of a group of scientists and engineers who volunteered to assist artists in incorporating modern technology into their work, a hallmark of the collaborative spirit between industry and the arts in the New York metropolitan area during that era.
